It is unnecessary to ask about the specific time of Christ's coming. Christ did not reveal this to the apostles. There are times and seasons for us to work in, and these are our duty and interest to know and observe; but as to the time when we must give an account of ourselves, we do not know it, nor is it necessary that we should. The coming of Christ will be a great surprise to people. Our Lord himself said so. Just as the hour of death is the same for each person as the judgment will be for mankind in general, the same remarks apply to both. Christ's coming will be terrible to those who are not devoted to God. Their destruction will overtake them while they dream of happiness and indulge themselves with frivolous amusements. There will be no way to escape the terror or punishment of that day. This day will be a happy day for those who are righteous. They are not in darkness; they are children of the light. It is the blessed condition of all true Christians. But how many people are speaking peace and safety to themselves, over whose heads destruction is looming! Let us strive to awaken ourselves and each other, and guard against our spiritual enemies.

Most people do not consider matters of another world at all, because they are asleep; or they do not consider them correctly, because they sleep and dream. Our moderation in all earthly things should be known by everyone. Should Christians, who have the light of the blessed gospel shining on their faces, be careless about their souls and unmindful of another world? We need the spiritual armor, or the three Christian virtues: faith, love, and hope. Faith; if we believe that God’s eye is always upon us, that there is another world to prepare for, we will see reason to watch and be sober. True and fervent love for God and things of God will keep us watchful and sober. If we have hope of salvation, let us take heed of anything that would shake our trust in the Lord. We have a foundation on which to build unshaken hope when we consider that salvation is through our Lord Jesus Christ, who died for us to atone for our sins and ransom our souls. We should join together in prayer and praise. We should set a good example for one another, and this is the best way to achieve the purpose of society. Thus we will learn how to live to Him, with whom we hope to live forever.

The ministers of the gospel are described by their work, which is to serve and honor the Lord. It is their duty not only to give good counsel but also to warn the flock of dangers and correct any wrongdoing. The people should honor and love their ministers because their concern is for the welfare of people’s souls. And the people should be at peace among themselves, doing all they can to prevent disagreements. But a desire for peace must not cause us to overlook sin. Those who are fearful and sorrowful should be encouraged, and a kind word may do much good. We must bear with each other and show patience, controlling our anger toward everyone. Whatever anyone does to us, we must do good to others.

We are to rejoice in earthly comforts as if we did not, and we must not expect to live many years and enjoy them all; but if we rejoice in God, we can do that forever. A truly religious life is a life of constant joy. And we should rejoice even more if we prayed more. Prayer will help every lawful endeavor and every good work. If we pray without ceasing, we will not lack reasons for giving thanks for everything. We will see cause to give thanks for God’s sparing and preventing grace, for common and uncommon, past and present, temporal and spiritual blessings. Not only for prosperous and pleasing things but also for difficult circumstances, for discipline and correction; because God designs all things for our good, even though we may not understand how at the time. Do not stifle the Spirit. Christians are said to be baptized with the Holy Ghost and with fire. He works like fire by enlightening, enlivening, and purifying people’s souls. Just as fire is extinguished by removing fuel or by pouring water on it or covering it with earth, we must be careful not to quench the Holy Spirit by indulging in carnal desires and focusing only on earthly things. Believers often hinder their spiritual growth by failing to give themselves over to the spiritual affections raised in their hearts by the Holy Spirit. By preaching the word, interpreting, and applying the Scriptures, we will learn how to live to Him, with whom we hope to live forever. We must not despise preaching, even if it is simple or we have heard it before. We must study the Scriptures and carefully examine everything so that we may hold fast to what is good. We should abstain from sin and anything that looks like sin, leads to it, or borders on it. Whoever avoids the appearance of sin, shuns opportunities for it, and avoids temptations and approaches to it will not long engage in sinful acts.

The apostle prays that they might be made holy even more completely, because even the best people are only partially sanctified while in this world; therefore we should pray for and strive toward complete holiness. And just as we would fall if God did not continue his work in our souls, we should pray to God to perfect his work until we are presented faultless before his glorious throne. We should pray for one another; and brothers should express brotherly love in this way. This letter was to be read to all the believers. Not only common people but everyone is allowed to read the Scriptures, and it is their duty to do so. The word of God should not be kept in an unknown language but translated so that as everyone is concerned to know the Scriptures, they may also be able to read them. The Scriptures should be read aloud in all public gatherings, especially for the benefit of those who are unlearned. We need nothing more to make us happy than to understand the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ. He is an unending and overflowing fountain of grace to supply every need.
